commentary: Eleven sessions this week versus twelve last week — one fewer, but honestly the shape of it is just as full, and that 13.1km run on Saturday at 159bpm versus last week's 151bpm tells a story all on its own: same distance, harder effort, more fire in the engine. The swim volume held beautifully at four sessions including three solid two-kilometre efforts at Prince Alfred, which is becoming very much your pool, your lane, your vibe. What's interesting is the double-up pattern — swim then weights on both Sunday and Monday, which is either incredibly disciplined or slightly unhinged, and either way it's very you. The one thing worth watching is that your recovery metrics are creeping — higher heart rates across similar efforts suggest your body is working harder to keep up with what you're asking of it, so the morning walk on Friday was actually the smartest thing you did all week, even if it felt like a cop-out.
